entities.name: GROUNDNUT
entities.description: The crop Arachis hypogaea L. studied for yield and soil property responses

entities.name: BIOCHAR
entities.description: A soil amendment used to improve soil properties and crop performance

entities.name: GYPSUM
entities.description: A soil amendment (CaSO4.2H2O) applied to study its effects on groundnut

entities.name: CHLOROPHYLL CONCENTRATION
entities.description: A measure of chlorophyll levels in groundnut plants

entities.name: WATER USE EFFICIENCY
entities.description: The efficiency with which the plant uses water, denoted as WUE

entities.name: BIOMASS YIELD
entities.description: The total biological mass produced by the groundnut plant

entities.name: SOIL PROPERTIES
entities.description: General characteristics of the soil being amended

entities.name: WATER STRESS
entities.description: The condition of limited water availability for the plant

entities.name: SPLIT-SPLIT-PLOT DESIGN
entities.description: The experimental design used to organize the application of amendments and water

entities.name: SOIL PH
entities.description: The measure of acidity or alkalinity of the soil

entities.name: CATION EXCHANGE CAPACITY
entities.description: The soil's ability to hold and exchange cations

entities.name: DRY MATTER YIELD
entities.description: The weight of the plant material after water removal

entities.name: MOISTURE RETENTION
entities.description: The ability of the soil to retain water

entities.name: PLANT WATER REQUIREMENT
entities.description: The daily water requirement of the plant, used to determine irrigation levels

relationships.description: Increases dry matter yield and improves performance
Studied for its effect on crop performance
Negatively impacts yield and physiological traits
Significantly raises soil pH from 5 to 7.15
Increases the soil's ability to retain moisture
Higher levels of PWR increase dry matter yields
Lower levels of PWR reduce WUE
Experimental framework used to evaluate the crop's response to treatments
Increases dry matter yield by 28% at 1 and 2% application rates
Affects the water use efficiency of the crop
